Appearances:
Attorney, Trang La of the law offices of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices LLP (415-626-7744) appears as counsel for plaintiff/ex parte applicant

Attorney, George King of the law offices of King, King & Zatkin (510-496-46451) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Lama Ashkar

Court apologizes to the parties for its impatience at previous proceedings.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

Court'S Own Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6

The Court vacates its June 7, 2019 order granting the ex parte application of Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP for entry of judgment pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.6 in the amount of $57,408.22, and grants Plaintiff's motion for entry of judgment in the stipulated settlement amount of $40,000.00. As Plaintiff evidently was aware when it entered into the Settlement Agreement and Release of Claims, the parties' settlement sought to impose an unenforceable penalty within the meaning of Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495. (See also, e.g., Purcell v. Schweitzer (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 969, 974-976.) Under the circumstances, entry of judgment in the stipulated amount is the appropriate remedy. (See Vivatech International, Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 796, 800, 815 [reversing and remanding for trial court to grant motion and enter a new judgment for the settlement amount, plus costs]; Greentree Financial Group, Inc., 163 Cal.App.4th at 498, 503 [same].) Written order and judgment are signed and filed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR No. 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Trang La, Esq., Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP. Telephone 415-626-7744, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal.

Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP's motion to set aside the order dismissing this case filed on November 20, 2018 is granted. The court notes that Defendant Lama Ashkar states that she was not served with the motion, and the address that appears on Plaintiff's proof of service appears to be incorrect (misspelled street name, wrong zip code). Plaintiff has shown that the order dismissing the case was entered as a result of counsel's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect due to a calendaring error and counsel's absence on maternity leave. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).) Although Plaintiff has not fully explained why it delayed as long as it did in bringing the motion, Defendant has not shown that she will be prejudiced by reinstating the action for the sole purpose of enabling Plaintiff to seek summary enforcement of the parties' settlement agreement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, assuming such relief is available to it and is warranted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
